content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 19, 1988

Kathleen Modugno, Treasurer
Friends of Coastal Open Space/Yes on
Proposition C
P.O. Box 91840
Santa Barbara, CA 93190

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-223

Dear Ms. Modugno:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTIONS

1. Were contributions received by Friends of Coastal Open
Space/Yes on Proposition C ("the Committee") correctly reported as
having been made by Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox
("Concerned Citizens")?

2. Is the Committee a sponsored committee?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Based on the information you have provided, it appears
that Concerned Citizens was correctly disclosed as the source of
the contributions. However, if you have information which
indicates otherwise, you should take reasonable steps to inquire
into the true source of the contributions.

2. The Committee may be sponsored by Concerned Citizens.
However, more information is needed before a determination can be
made.

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Kathleen Modugno
Page Two

FACTS

Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox, an unincorporated
association, was awarded $30,000 in settlement of a lawsuit with
Santa Barbara Senior Housing Associates, Inc. The settlement
specified that the $30,000 was to be used on a campaign to support
Proposition C in the City of Santa Barbara. Through the period
ending May 21, 1988, Concerned Citizens had contributed $7,930 to
the Friends of Coastal Open Space/Yes on Proposition C Committee.
At that time, the $7,930 represented 40 percent of the Committee's
total receipts. Additional contributions could increase Concerned
Citizen's percentage to 70 percent of total receipts.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires committees formed to support or oppose ballot
measures to file the campaign disclosure reports set forth in
Section 84200 et seq. On those reports, such committees must
provide detailed information regarding persons from whom they have
received contributions totaling $100 or more during the calendar
year. (Section 84211.)

There is nothing in the facts you presented to indicate that
someone other than Concerned Citizens should be listed as the
source of the contributions received by the Committee. However,
committee treasurers have an obligation to use all reasonable
diligence in determining the true source of contributions, and are
required by Commission regulations to:

Correct any inaccuracies or omissions in campaign statements
of which the treasurer knows, and cause to be checked, and if,
necessary, corrected, any information in campaign statements
which a person of reasonable prudence would question based on
all the surrounding circumstances of which the treasurer is
aware or should be aware by reason of his or her duties under
this regulation and the Act.

Regulation 18427.

If you have reason to believe that Concerned Citizens is
making contributions to the Committee under circumstances other
than the ones you described, you should seek additional
information from them. In addition, please note that Section
84211 requires the Committee to disclose, among other things, the
name and address of each contributor of $100 or more. If the
contribution was received through an intermediary, Section 84302
requires disclosure of the name and address of the intermediary as
well.

Kathleen Modugno
Page Three


With regard to your second question, Section 82048.5 states
that an entity or organization sponsors a committee if any of the
following apply:

     1.  The committee receives all or nearly all of its
contributions from the entity or organization or its members,
officers, employees or shareholders; or

     2.  The entity or organization collects contributions
for the committee by use of payroll deductions or dues from
its members, officers or employees; or

     3.  The entity or organization, alone or in combination
with other organizations, provides all or nearly all of the
administrative services for the committee; or

     4.  The entity or organization, alone or in combination
with other organizations, sets the policies for soliciting
contributions or making expenditures of committee funds.

Your letter provides insufficient facts to determine if
Concerned Citizens sponsors the Committee. Please feel free to
call me at (916) 322-5662 if you would like to discuss this
question, or if you need additional information.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]

By                                      Carla Wardlow
                                        Political Reform Consultant

JUL 1 | [illegible] FRIENDS OF COASTAL OPEN SPACE/
YES ON PROPOSITION C [illegible]
P.O. BOX 91840
SANTA BARBARA, CA 93190


May 30, 1988

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear FPPC representative,

This letter is in regard to contributions our committee has
received from Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox, an
unincorporated association. We have received contributions of
$7,930 from this committee on our Form 420 for the period ended
May 21, 1988 (see pg 6 of 9).

Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox was awarded $30,000 in
settlement of a lawsuit with Santa Barbara Senior Housing
Associates Inc. The settlement specified that the $30,000 was to
be used on a campaign for Proposition C. As of May 29, our
committee has received $7,930 from the fiduciary holding the
settlement funds.

Our concerns are twofold. Firstly, we want to fully disclose the
nature of the contribution on our campaign reports. If our
method of disclosure is not complete, please let us know how to
amend our report.

Secondly, we are concerned about our potential status as a
sponsored committee as a result of receiving the contributions
from Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox. At the present time
those contributions represent 40% of our total cash receipts. If
we receive more contributions the percentage could go up to 70%
or more. Will you please respond to this concern?

Thank you for your help in these matters.


Sincerely,

Kathleen Modugno
Kathleen Modugno
Treasurer


Enclosure
